engineIntelligence.h2: Chrysler Delta Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Chrysler Delta in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Chrysler Delta 198A2.000 engine - diesel 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve clogging - causes rough idle and restricted airflow on short urban journeys.
Injector seal wear - leads to oil contamination and black sludge buildup around the valve cover.

cards.altText: Chrysler Delta 198A4.000 engine - petrol 1.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo oil feed coking - delayed oil changes starve the turbo, causing shaft play and boost leaks.
Ignition coil failure - leads to intermittent misfires and rough running under load.

cards.altText: Chrysler Delta 940A2.000 engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Dual-mass flywheel wear - high torque loads cause internal spring failure and severe clutch shudder.
Turbo actuator sticking - results in inconsistent boost pressure and limp mode activation.

commonProblems.h2: Chrysler Delta Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Chrysler Delta’s engine lineup is generally robust, but specific vulnerabilities emerge as mileage increases. The 1.4 T-Jet’s turbocharger issues and the MultiJet diesel’s DPF and EGR complications dominate UK replacement demand. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this guide to weigh your repair versus replacement options.

problems.h4: 1.4 T-Jet Turbocharger Failure - Why Chrysler Delta Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil feed pipes coke up due to delayed oil changes, starving the turbo bearings and causing shaft play and boost leaks.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo and upgraded oil feed pipe.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if the engine block is healthy and oil changes were regular.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components.
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution if turbo failure caused engine damage.

problems.recommendation: If caught early with no engine knocking, a standalone turbo replacement is cost-effective. However, if the engine has suffered oil starvation, a full rebuilt unit is the safest investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1.4 T-Jet engine prices for your Chrysler Delta

problems.h4: MultiJet DPF & EGR Blockage - Why Chrysler Delta Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Frequent short journeys prevent the DPF from regenerating, while the EGR valve accumulates carbon, leading to restricted airflow and limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical cleaning of DPF and EGR valve.
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; blockage often returns if driving habits don't change.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned engine with cleaned emissions systems.
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution restoring full power and MOT compliance.

problems.recommendation: If the engine is otherwise healthy, professional DPF/EGR cleaning is a smart preventative measure. If the blockage has caused catastrophic overheating, replacement is unavoidable.
problems.cta: Get quotes for MultiJet engine replacement for your Chrysler Delta

problems.h4: 2.0 MultiJet Dual-Mass Flywheel (DMF) Failure - Why Chrysler Delta Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: The high torque output of the 2.0L engine places immense stress on the DMF, causing internal springs to wear and creating severe clutch shudder and rattling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace DMF, clutch assembly, and release bearing.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores smooth gear changes for another 80k+ miles if engine is sound.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install rebuilt 2.0L engine complete with new DMF and clutch.
repairOptions.longevity: The ultimate reset for high-mileage performance diesel models.

problems.recommendation: A standalone DMF replacement is standard practice if the engine runs smoothly. However, if the rattling is accompanied by metallic debris in the oil, a full engine replacement is required.
problems.cta: Find supply and fit deals for your 2.0 MultiJet Chrysler Delta

problems.h4: Timing Belt Snap & Interference Damage - Why Chrysler Delta Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: All Chrysler Delta variants (2011-2014, all codes)
problems.rootCause: The Delta features interference engines; a snapped or jumped timing belt causes pistons to strike open valves, bending them and destroying the cylinder head.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace belt, tensioners, and water pump at recommended intervals.
repairOptions.longevity: Highly recommended preventative maintenance every 5 years or 60k miles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit a reconditioned engine with a brand-new timing kit.
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if the belt has snapped, as head repairs are rarely economical.

problems.recommendation: Never ignore the manufacturer’s timing belt service interval. If the belt has snapped, do not attempt to start the engine; a full rebuilt unit is the only reliable path forward.
problems.cta: Secure a warrantied replacement engine for your Chrysler Delta today

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chrysler Delta
finalCta.paragraph: Ignoring early warning signs like turbo rattle, DPF limp mode, or clutch shudder can turn a manageable repair into a total engine loss. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Chrysler Delta specialists, ensuring you get a fair deal. All our network suppliers provide upgraded components to fix known factory flaws, nationwide delivery, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Chrysler Delta Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chrysler Delta and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Chrysler Delta Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: Use this Chrysler Delta year guide to match your vehicle to the right engine family, production period, and replacement options.

years.description: The first year of the Chrysler-badged Delta in the UK introduced the refined MultiJet and T-Jet engine families to British roads.
keyChanges: Introduction of the Chrysler Delta to the UK market as a rebadged Lancia Delta (Type 844).
Standard fitment of the 1.4 T-Jet, 1.6 MultiJet, and 2.0 MultiJet engines.
Establishment of SE, S, and Limited trim levels.
knownFor: Raw, unrefined American branding on an Italian chassis.
Early 1.6 MultiJet units exhibiting the first wave of DPF regeneration issues.
High import rarity means parts can sometimes require cross-referencing with Lancia Delta.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011 Chrysler Delta replacement engines
years.ticker: 2011 Chrysler Delta 1.6 diesel engine replacement • 2011 Delta 1.4 T-Jet turbo failure

years.description: The 2012 and 2013 model years brought subtle but crucial updates to the engine management software and introduced higher-output variants to the lineup.
keyChanges: Introduction of the 940A8.000 1.6L MultiJet (120bhp) variant for select trims.
Rollout of the 198A7.000 1.4L T-Jet with MultiAir technology.
Updated ECU calibrations to improve emissions and fuel economy.
knownFor: Slightly improved reliability over the 2011 models due to running changes.
Continued vulnerability to MultiAir solenoid faults if oil change intervals are extended.
High search volume for "2012 Chrysler Delta 1.6 diesel engine replacement".
years.cta: Compare prices for 2012-2013 Chrysler Delta engine replacements
years.ticker: 2012 Chrysler Delta 1.6 MultiJet replacement • 2013 Delta 1.4 MultiAir engine fix

years.description: The final year of Chrysler Delta production in the UK featured the most refined iterations of both diesel and petrol engines before the model was discontinued.
keyChanges: Final refinement of the 1.6L and 2.0L MultiJet engines with updated sensor arrays.
End of production for the Chrysler Delta, cementing its status as a rare UK import.
Last availability of the premium 2.0 MultiJet 190 AWD variant.
knownFor: Best overall reliability of the Delta lineup due to final-year manufacturing tweaks.
High desirability for low-mileage, rebuilt examples to keep these rare hatchbacks on the road.
Strong cross-compatibility with late-model Lancia Delta units.
years.cta: Secure a warrantied 2014 Chrysler Delta replacement engine today
years.ticker: 2014 Chrysler Delta 2.0 diesel replacement • 2014 Delta engine rebuild vs replace

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Chrysler Delta was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Chrysler Delta specialists.

faq.h2: Chrysler Delta Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Chrysler Delta engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A Chrysler Delta engine replacement typically costs between £1,100 and £2,800 for a rebuilt unit, depending on whether you have the 1.4L, 1.6L, or 2.0L engine. Used options start around £600, while full supply and fit packages will add £500-£900 in labour.
keyPoints: 1.4L rebuilt engines average £1,100-£2,200.
1.6L and 2.0L rebuilt engines average £1,200-£2,800.
Supply and fit labour adds £500-£900.
All rebuilt units include a 12-month warranty.
items.cta: Compare Chrysler Delta engine replacement prices today

items.question: Can I put a Lancia Delta engine in a Chrysler Delta?
items.answer: Yes, the Chrysler Delta is essentially a rebadged Lancia Delta (Type 844), meaning cross-compatibility is excellent. As long as the engine code and model year match, a Lancia engine will fit perfectly.
keyPoints: Chrysler and Lancia Deltas share the exact same platform and mounting points.
Engine codes must match exactly for ECU compatibility.
Wiring harnesses and sensor arrays are fully interchangeable.
Always verify the VIN before purchasing a cross-platform unit.
items.cta: Get a Chrysler Delta engine compatibility check now

items.question: What are the signs of Chrysler Delta engine failure?
items.answer: Key warning signs include a loud turbo rattle on acceleration, DPF limp mode with reduced power, rough idling from EGR clogging, and illuminated check engine lights for misfires.
keyPoints: Turbo rattle indicates wastegate wear or oil feed coking.
Limp mode points to severe DPF or EGR blockage.
Blue exhaust smoke signals severe oil burning or injector seal failure.
A deep knocking sound indicates catastrophic bottom-end failure.
items.cta: Find a reliable replacement engine for your Chrysler Delta

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Chrysler Delta?
items.answer: Yes, replacing the engine is highly viable and often more economical than purchasing another vehicle, given the Delta's robust chassis and the availability of affordable, warrantied reconditioned units.
keyPoints: A rebuilt engine costs significantly less than a new car.
The Delta's chassis and drivetrain are durable and long-lasting.
Reconditioned units fix factory flaws like DPF vulnerabilities.
Extends the vehicle's life by another 80,000+ miles.
items.cta: Request a Chrysler Delta rebuilt engine quote today

items.question: How long does a rebuilt Chrysler Delta engine last?
items.answer: A professionally rebuilt Chrysler Delta engine can easily exceed 80,000 to 100,000 miles when paired with strict adherence to service intervals, especially using high-quality synthetic oil to protect the turbo and MultiAir systems.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines replace all critical wear items.
Upgraded DPF and EGR components prevent early failure.
Regular 10,000-mile oil changes are critical for longevity.
Backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

items.question: Does a Chrysler Delta engine replacement include a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every engine sourced through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Supply and fit packages often include comprehensive parts and labour coverage.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty on all rebuilt units.
Unlimited mileage coverage for total peace of mind.
Supply and fit includes labour warranty.
Warranty terms are clearly stated by each UK specialist.

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Chrysler Delta engines?
items.answer: While often used interchangeably, "reconditioned" typically implies a more thorough restoration to exact factory tolerances, whereas "rebuilt" may only replace the specifically failed components and adjacent wear items.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines are fully stripped and machined.
Rebuilt engines target specific failure points (e.g., turbo, injectors).
Both options are vastly superior to untested used engines.
Reconditioned units command a slight premium but offer maximum longevity.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Chrysler Delta engine prices

items.question: How long does Chrysler Delta engine supply and fit take?
items.answer: A typical Chrysler Delta engine supply and fit process takes 3 to 7 working days, covering sourcing, nationwide delivery, professional installation, and final ECU calibration and road testing.
keyPoints: Sourcing a matched engine takes 1-3 days.
Nationwide delivery adds 1-2 days.
Professional installation requires 1-2 days.
Includes post-fit diagnostics and DPF adaptation resetting.
items.cta: Book Chrysler Delta engine supply and fit today

items.question: What causes the 1.4 T-Jet turbo failure in the Chrysler Delta?
items.answer: Turbo failure is primarily caused by delayed oil changes, leading to coked oil feed pipes that starve the turbo bearings, subsequently causing shaft play, boost leaks, and eventual turbo destruction.
keyPoints: Turbo relies on a constant supply of clean, cool oil.
Coked oil pipes restrict flow, causing rapid bearing wear.
Wastegate rattle is often the first warning sign of failure.
Upgraded oil feed pipes are a common rebuild solution.
items.cta: Compare prices for rebuilt 1.4 T-Jet engines for your Delta

items.question: Can I get a remanufactured engine for my Chrysler Delta?
items.answer: Yes, while rare, we can source or commission remanufactured units for customers demanding absolute, zero-mile reliability that exceeds original factory specifications.
keyPoints: Remanufactured engines are blueprinted to exact tolerances.
All internal components are brand new or freshly machined.
Ideal for owners planning to keep their Delta indefinitely.
Availability is limited, so early enquiry is recommended.
